context_docs.title: History of Independence Palace
context_docs.content: Independence Palace, a symbol of power often compared to the 'White House' of the former South Vietnam. Construction began in 1962 on the site of the old Norodom Palace... Today, it is not only a special national monument with a solid underground bunker system and luxurious reception rooms, but also a venue for important diplomatic events.

name: Dragon Wharf
vision_tags: two porcelain dragons on the roof
Ho Chi Minh statue in the courtyard

context_docs.title: History of Dragon Wharf
context_docs.content: Dragon Wharf was conceptualized and built between 1863 and 1864, originally serving as the headquarters for the French shipping company Messageries Maritimes. Its characteristic architecture features two dragons on the roof... Since 1975, this monument complex has been developed into the Ho Chi Minh Memorial Area.

name: Notre-Dame Cathedral Basilica of Saigon
vision_tags: statue of Our Lady of Peace
tall iron spires and bell towers

context_docs.title: History of Notre-Dame Cathedral
context_docs.content: Notre-Dame Cathedral Basilica of Saigon stands tall as a masterpiece of Romanesque and Gothic architecture, serving as an irreplaceable religious and cultural symbol of the city since construction began in 1877... this cathedral remains a 'precious gem' that captures the flow of time and the soul of Saigon across generations.

name: Ho Chi Minh City Fine Arts Museum

context_docs.title: Ho Chi Minh City Fine Arts Museum
context_docs.content: The Ho Chi Minh City Fine Arts Museum is a must-visit destination, formerly the magnificent residence of Hua Bon Hoa... Today, with a massive collection of over 22,000 artifacts, this heritage space not only preserves aesthetic values but also tells a vivid story of the development of Southern fine arts.

name: War Remnants Museum
vision_tags: military aircraft and helicopters on display

context_docs.title: History of War Remnants Museum
context_docs.content: The War Remnants Museum serves as a deeply authentic 'visual diary' of the Vietnamese people's resistance war against America... Rising above the pain, the museum today has become a symbol of a peace-loving spirit, reminding the world of the value of reconciliation and the powerful rebirth of a nation from the ashes of war.

context_docs.title: History of Ben Thanh Market
context_docs.content: The vibrant symbol known today as Ben Thanh Market once had a humble beginning along the banks of Cho Vai canal... Since that historic milestone, the name Ben Thanh has been associated with the new market and its famous four-faced clock tower.

context_docs.title: History of Bitexco Financial Tower
context_docs.content: Bitexco Financial Tower is a symbol of the rapid economic development of Ho Chi Minh City, with a design inspired by the shape of a lotus bud... With unique architecture featuring a helipad resembling an opening lotus blossom, the tower is not only a world-class structure but also a testament to the creativity and towering vision of the city.

name: Saigon Central Post Office
vision_tags: giant clock above the main entrance
rows of symmetrical windows and balconies
iron arch and steel column interior system
large portrait of Ho Chi Minh inside
painted maps on the indoor walls

context_docs.title: History of Saigon Central Post Office
context_docs.content: Saigon Central Post Office (formerly the Saigon Telegraph Office) was initiated by the French on November 11, 1860... After over 130 years of history, with a major renovation in 2014, the building is now not only a postal center but also an iconic architectural monument and a famous tourist attraction in Ho Chi Minh City.

name: Nguyen Hue Walking Street
vision_tags: City People's Committee Headquarters in the background
surrounded by tall modern buildings and hotels

context_docs.title: History of Nguyen Hue Walking Street
context_docs.content: Nguyen Hue Walking Street began its history as a small canal that was converted into the Cho Vai canal to serve trade... In 1955, the street was officially renamed Nguyen Hue Boulevard, and by 2015, it became Vietnam's first pedestrian street, continuing its tradition as a vital cultural and festive space for the city.

name: Turtle Lake
vision_tags: surrounded by green trees and cafes

context_docs.title: History of Turtle Lake
context_docs.content: Turtle Lake originated in 1790, originally located at the Kham Khuyet gate of the Bat Quai Citadel... Although the turtle statue and the stele were destroyed by an explosion in 1976 (or 1978), the name has been preserved and remains widely used by locals to this day.

context_docs.title: History of Thu Ngu Flag Tower
context_docs.content: The Thu Ngu Flagpole (French name: Mât des Signaux) was built in October 1865 on the old site of the defensive mandarin's mansion... After existing for more than 150 years, the flagpole was classified as a city-level historical monument in 2016 and underwent major restoration in late 2020.

context_docs.title: History of Tao Dan Park
context_docs.content: Tao Dan Park began its history as part of the French Governor General's Palace grounds. In 1869, this area was separated with the official name Jardin de la Ville... Since 1975, it has been named Tao Dan Cultural Park, serving not only as a 'green lung' but also as a vital outdoor cultural, artistic, and recreational space for the residents.

name: Saigon Zoo and Botanical Gardens
vision_tags: yellow main gate with French architecture
very tall and thick ancient trees
History Museum building inside the grounds

context_docs.title: History of Saigon Zoo and Botanical Gardens
context_docs.content: Saigon Zoo and Botanical Gardens, originally poetically known as the Botanical Garden, exists as a 'living museum' in the heart of the city... After 1975, this area has continuously transformed, expanding its conservation space to become an invaluable cultural and biodiversity heritage, a peaceful rendezvous connecting the past and the present.

context_docs.title: History of Vinh Nghiem Pagoda
context_docs.content: Vinh Nghiem Pagoda was formed from the noble aspiration of two venerable monks... With its curved tiled roofs and wooden columns bearing the distinct traditional mark of the North, the pagoda is not only a symbol of modern Buddhism in the South but also a magical spiritual bridge.

name: Starlight Bridge
vision_tags: LED lights on the bridge surface

context_docs.title: History of Starlight Bridge
context_docs.content: Starlight Bridge appears as a brilliantly glowing silk strip in the heart of the Phu My Hung urban area, marking a significant milestone as the first pedestrian bridge in Vietnam during the 2009-2010 period... Not just an engineering masterpiece, it has become a symbol of modern rhythm and a signature romantic rendezvous, where humanity and nature harmonize in light.
